abstract: Asserts if the lock object fails to meet specified ownership requirements.
overview:
## Discussion

Call this function to ensure the ownership state of the lock is what your code requires. For example, if you call this function and pass [doc://com.apple.os/documentation/os/OSAllocatedUnfairLock/Ownership/owner], the app terminates if the object is locked, but the calling code doesn’t own the lock. Similarly, if you call it and pass [doc://com.apple.os/documentation/os/OSAllocatedUnfairLock/Ownership/notOwner], the app terminates if the calling code doesn’t own the lock, or if the object isn’t locked.

# precondition(_:)

**Instance Method**

Asserts if the lock object fails to meet specified ownership requirements.

## Declaration

```swift
func precondition(_ condition: OSAllocatedUnfairLock<State>.Ownership)
```

## Parameters

- **condition**: The ownership status to check.

## Discussion

Call this function to ensure the ownership state of the lock is what your code requires. For example, if you call this function and pass [doc://com.apple.os/documentation/os/OSAllocatedUnfairLock/Ownership/owner], the app terminates if the object is locked, but the calling code doesn’t own the lock. Similarly, if you call it and pass [doc://com.apple.os/documentation/os/OSAllocatedUnfairLock/Ownership/notOwner], the app terminates if the calling code doesn’t own the lock, or if the object isn’t locked.

## Determining lock ownership

- **OSAllocatedUnfairLock.Ownership**: An enumeration that represents the ownership status of an unfair lock.
